sourceMovie.title: Detective Conan: The Last Wizard of the Century
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Detective Conan: The Last Wizard of the Century (1999) stuck because it commits to its adventure premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Kenji Kodama's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The adventure and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lupin III: The First
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Last Wizard of the Century clicked for you, Lupin III: The First (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Last Wizard of the Century clicked for you, Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and animation, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Detective Conan: The Last Wizard of the Century?
faq.answer: Try Lupin III: The First, Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target, Case Closed: The Fist of Blue Sapphire—each shares DNA with Detective Conan: The Last Wizard of the Century while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Detective Conan: The Last Wizard of the Century?
faq.answer: Detective Conan: The Last Wizard of the Century (1999) is best described as Adventure, Animation, Crime, Drama, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Detective Conan: The Last Wizard of the Century part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Detective Conan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
